data.title: Investor Hakimian warns U.S. economy under Trump's 2nd term is struggling with growth from government spending, tech expansion.
data.description: Hedge fund manager Spencer Hakimian has raised concerns about the state of the U.S. economy during President Donald Trump's second term. In a recent post, Hakimian described the economy as "barely hanging on by a string" six months into "Trump 2.0", attributing any growth to significant government spending increasing by over 10% annually, as well as the unrestricted expansion of big tech companies. He highlighted that the economy is mainly being driven by Washington DC and San Francisco, with other regions and sectors facing pressure. Hakimian emphasized the reliance on government spending and big tech, noting the challenges faced by other sectors. He also mentioned that the country is witnessing unchecked growth in these areas, indicating potential risks.

data.title: Arbitrum's price set to break $0.58 despite short-term risks. Forecast predicts $0.39 support, aiming $0.58-$1.24 by Sep 2025.
data.description: There are conflicting signals in the ARB price prediction, indicating a potential decrease to the $0.39 support level in the near term. However, the medium-term forecast for Arbitrum anticipates a range of $0.58 to $1.24 by the end of the month.

data.title: OpenAI partners with Walmart to train 10 million Americans in Generative AI by 2030, pilot launching 2025.
data.description: OpenAI recently announced the launch of a generative artificial intelligence certification program due to the rising demand for AI skills in businesses. Teaming up with Walmart Inc. (NYSE: WMT), they aim to certify 10 million Americans by 2030. The program builds on the educational resources provided by OpenAI Academy, with Walmart's 2 million employees receiving specialized certification versions. Donna Morris, Walmart's Chief People Officer, confirmed the tailored certifications. The pilot certification program is set to begin by late 2025 or early 2026, alongside the development of a dedicated jobs platform.

data.title: Lenovo unveils AI-powered devices at Innovation World 2025, including high-performance PCs and Motorola smartphones.
data.description: At the Lenovo Innovation World 2025 event in Berlin, Lenovo unveiled its newest array of AI-driven technology products. This includes advanced PCs, smart tablets, cutting-edge gaming devices, and Motorola smartphones. These innovations showcase Lenovo's commitment to making AI more accessible to everyone, incorporating generative AI and hybrid intelligence into daily tasks, creative endeavors, and entertainment. The lineup includes adaptive devices, AI-compatible workstations, handheld gaming consoles, creator tablets, and more.
